contextBanner: [TBG | Sprint 7 | Skill Router v2 | harness/agent-routing | runtime-observer]
purpose: Select the narrowest primary skill from request intent, touched paths, commands, artifacts, proof request, and risk; enforce declared authority, validation, artifact, freshness, and proof boundaries before work begins; route completed runtime-observer incidents read-only without merging incident triage into launcher control or promoting proof.
rules: Read AGENTS.md before selecting a skill.
Select exactly one primary skill unless the request is too ambiguous to perform safely.
Route process loss, external terminal evidence, open spans at process loss, heartbeat stalls, and observer gaps to runtime-incident-triage.
Route window error or unknown quarantine to window-lifecycle-runtime, runtime observer start/status to launcher-lifecycle, and incident_ready to runtime-evidence-certification.
Load every requiresSkills entry and no unrelated skill.
Load composesWith entries only when the request genuinely crosses that boundary.
Reject an exclusiveWith combination unless a higher-authority workflow partitions the work into separate lanes.
Do not exceed the primary skill's proofCeiling.
Do not write outside ownedPaths or inside forbiddenPaths.
Resolve mutable state from freshnessSource and currentStateSources rather than historical prose.
Run every practical validator registered for the changed lane.
Produce or explicitly account for every expected artifact before closeout.
allowedScope: edit skill cards, manifest, schema, router contract, and routing validator
register read-only runtime-observer capabilities and artifact-engine trigger routes
read current source and current generated state for route classification
forbiddenScope: claim launcher, behavior, or live runtime proof from routing validation
start, stop, restart, kill, click, focus, or save through a trigger
delete branches, worktrees, pull requests, or evidence

requires: AGENTS.md contains only universal boot, safety, proof, routing, current-state pointer, encoding, and closeout rules
mutable pull request restrictions and the current strategic target are absent from AGENTS.md
every skill declares match signals, dependencies, composition, exclusion, entry contract, authorities, validators, artifacts, proof ceiling, risk, freshness, owned paths, and forbidden paths
every registered skill file exists and contains the standard routing sections
the manifest does not route through the superseded post-PR41 floor snapshot
runtime-observer incident routes are read-only and do not grant cleanup, restart, click, save, or proof-promotion authority
the PowerShell routing validator carries a UTF-8 BOM and parses under Windows PowerShell 5.1
